figure.title: Error Detected at Accelerator UALink TL
figure.purpose: Shows the system state when an error is detected at the UALink TL of an Accelerator.

technical_summary: An error is detected within the UALink TL of ACC A.
The UALink TL and the corresponding UPLI Originator in ACC A are placed into an isolation or error-handling state (highlighted with dashed boxes).
A loopback or isolation symbol (purple arrow) is shown at the UPLI Originator, indicating traffic from the upper layer is being dropped or looped back.

components.name: UALink TL (ACC A)
components.description: The layer where the error is detected.

components.name: UPLI Originator (ACC A)
components.description: Enters isolation mode in response to the TL error.

critical_facts: Errors detected at the UALink TL of an accelerator propagate up to the UPLI Originator, causing it to enter isolation mode to prevent further traffic generation.
